title: Modern Farmhouse Coffee Table
summary: A shelf-and-top coffee table on angled 2x4 legs with corner brackets — 1x10 (or plywood) panels trimmed in 1x4, joined by a 2x4 cap and mitered angled leg supports.
description:
This coffee table builds two nearly identical panels — a top and a bottom shelf — from 1x10 boards trimmed out in 1x4, then joins them with angled 2x4 legs braced by 45°-cut corner supports. Building both panels to match exactly is worth checking directly: cut the long trim pieces to fit your actual assembled panel width rather than a plan number, since edge-joined boards rarely land on a precise calculated width, and a mismatched pair of panels will read as crooked once the legs go on.

A genuinely useful material substitution is built into the plan itself: swap the 1x10 tops for an off-the-shelf edge-glued panel or plywood of your choice, and in humid climates, use 3/4" plywood or MDF instead of the 1x10s, since solid softwood boards swell more with humidity than manufactured panels do.

Attach the legs to the bottom shelf first, checking that they're square to the panel with a carpenter's square before adding the angled corner supports — those supports are both bracing and a design element, and their angle only looks intentional if the legs underneath are already true. Assemble upside down on its side on a level surface for the final step, setting the legs inside the top panel and gluing and nailing from there; corner brackets add a hidden mechanical connection alongside the glue joint for a coffee table that will see regular bumps and shifting.

materials.name: 1x10, 8 ft
materials.note: Or substitute an edge-glued panel or plywood.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Two boards joined edge to edge per panel; may substitute plywood or MDF panels.
Measure and cut to fit your assembled panel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45°, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Build the shelf and top panels
steps.body: If using 1x10s, join along the long edge with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the end trim with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the long trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the long trim pieces to fit your assembled panel, then nail and glue to the top and ends. Build the top and shelf panel to match exactly.

steps.title: Attach the legs to the shelf
steps.body: Cut the leg caps to match your end trim length. Attach the legs to the bottom shelf with 2" brad nails and wood glue, checking that the legs are square to the panel.

steps.title: Add the angled corner supports
steps.body: Attach the angled corner supports at each leg, checking that the legs stay square to the shelf.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Place the coffee table on its side on a level surface with the legs set inside the top panel. Attach with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, plus corner brackets for a secure mechanical connection.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and finish with primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Coffee table with a 1x10 top and shelf on angled 2x4 legs
